children.heading: Definition of high-need public secondary school
children.label: a Definition of high-need public secondary school

children.text: in which 40 percent or more of the students attending the school are children from low-income families; or
designated with a school locale code of 41, 42, or 43, as determined by the Secretary of Education.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: b Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish at each of the National Laboratories a program to support a Center of Excellence in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (referred to in this section as a “Center of Excellence”) in at least 1 high-need public secondary school located in the region served by the National Laboratory to provide assistance in accordance with subsection (f).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To comply with subsection (g), each high-need public secondary school selected as a Center of Excellence and the National Laboratory shall form a partnership with a school, department, or program of education at an institution of higher education.

children.heading: Nonprofit entities
children.label: 2 Nonprofit entities
children.text: The partnership may include a nonprofit entity with demonstrated experience and effectiveness in science or mathematics, as agreed to by other members of the partnership.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director, shall establish criteria to guide the National Laboratories in selecting the sites for Centers of Excellence.

children.heading: Process
children.label: 2 Process
children.text: A National Laboratory shall select a site for a Center of Excellence through an open, widely-publicized, and competitive process.

children.heading: Goals
children.label: e Goals
children.text: The Secretary shall establish goals and performance assessments for each Center of Excellence authorized under subsection (b).
to assist teachers in teaching a course at a Center of Excellence in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics; and
to use National Laboratory scientific equipment in the teaching of the course.

provision of clinical practicum, student teaching, or internship experiences for science, technology, and mathematics teacher candidates as part of the teacher preparation program of the Center of Excellence;
provision of supervision and mentoring for teacher candidates in the teacher preparation program; and
to the maximum extent practicable, provision of professional development for veteran teachers in the public secondary schools in the region.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: h Evaluation
children.text: The Secretary shall consider the results of performance assessments required under subsection (e) in determining the contract award fee of a National Laboratory management and operations contractor.
develop an evaluation and accountability plan for the activities funded under this section that objectively measures the impact of the activities; and
disseminate information obtained from those measurements.

children.heading: No effect on similar programs
children.label: j No effect on similar programs
children.text: Nothing in this section displaces or otherwise affects any similar program being carried out as of , at any National Laboratory under any other provision of law.August 9, 2007
